steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage says wooden objects have a "high susceptibility to rot," and the discovery of a 2,000-year-old wooden figure is described as "truly surprising." The word "but" signals a contrast: the blank must explain why survival is unusual. The blank needs an adverb meaning "infrequently" or "not often."

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — "sturdily": Means strongly or robustly. If wooden objects sturdily survived long periods, the 2,000-year preservation would not be surprising at all — it contradicts the passage's claim that wood is susceptible to rot. ✗
  • Choice B — "carelessly": Means accidentally or without care. The passage makes no claim about how wood survives — only how rarely it does. "Carelessly survive" introduces an unrelated idea of accidental preservation. ✗
  • Choice C — "rarely": Means infrequently. Wooden objects rarely survive long because they are highly susceptible to rot — which is exactly why a 2,000-year-old specimen is "truly surprising." ✓
  • Choice D — "simply": Means merely or just. "Simply survive" implies a baseline or ordinary survival, not an infrequent one, so it would not explain why the discovery is surprising. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
